macro_segments.title: Introduction to Perek Kol HaZevachim and the Topic of Ta'arovet (Mixtures)

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and overview of Daf 71, Perek Kol HaZevachim
Ta'arovet as a broader halakhic concept across multiple sugyot

macro_segments.title: The Five Categories of Chataot HaMetot and Their Mixed Status

micro_segments.title: The mnemonic ותמנ״ע for the five types of Chataot HaMetot that must die
Detailed explanation of each category: Vlat Chatat, Tmurat Chatat, Metu Ba'aleha, Niskapru Ba'acher, Avra Shnatah
When one forbidden animal (Shor HaNiskal or Chatat HaMetot) mixes with a flock of permitted Korbanot, all must die

macro_segments.title: Why Bitul Does Not Apply to Live Animals; Redemption and Benefit Prohibitions

micro_segments.title: Why ביטול (nullification) does not apply to mixed live animals; they remain important individually
Animals that are אסורי הנאה cannot be redeemed or benefited from by laypersons
Examples of animals that cannot be used as Korbanot but may be eaten by laypersons

macro_segments.title: Specific Cases of Forbidden Animals: Sin, Bestiality, Idolatry, and Halachic Restrictions

micro_segments.title: Shor She'Ne'evda Bo Aveira: ox used in sin; one witness vs. two witnesses distinction
Be'Rove'a Ve'Nirva: animals used in bestiality; difference between one and two witnesses
Be'Muktzeh and Ve'Ne'evad: idolatry-related animals; live animals cannot become Assur Be'Hana'ah
Be'Esnan U'Mechier, Be'Kilayim, U'Be'Treifa: prostitute payment, hybrids, and diseased animals
Be'Yotze Dofen: Cesarean section births from Pasuk Shor Kesev O Ez Ki Yivaled

macro_segments.title: Solutions for Mixed Forbidden Animals with Permitted Korbanot Through Mum and Redemption

micro_segments.title: General principle: let mixed animals graze until they develop a Mum (blemish)
Selling blemished animals and transferring Kedusha to money; keeping the value of the forbidden animal
Bi'Dmei Ha'Yafeh: assuming higher-valued animals were Kodshim; keeping only the lowest value
Special case of Treifa: selling for reduced price (dog food); still salvageable

macro_segments.title: Mixed Chullin with Kodshim; Mixed Kodshim of Different Types

micro_segments.title: Nis'arvu Chullin Bi'Tmimim: mixing non-sanctified with sanctified; finding buyer for the Chullin
Kodshim bekodshim min bemino: mixing identical types (e.g., Reuven's Olah with Shimon's Olah)
Kodshim bekodshim min beshe'eino mino: mixing different types (Olah and Shelamim); two-pile solution
Assigning higher values to presumed Kodshim; using two piles of money to separate Olah and Shelamim

macro_segments.title: Mixed Olah with Bechor or Ma'aser; Eating with Restrictions

micro_segments.title: Nitarvu bevochor uvima'aser: Olah mixed with Bechor or Ma'aser; cannot redeem the latter
Solution: let animals graze until Mum; redeemed Olah brought separately; remaining eaten as Bechor/Ma'aser
Yei'achel Kebechor Uchemaser: eating with Bechor restrictions (not in marketplace, not for full market price)

macro_segments.title: Exceptions: Chatat and Asham Cannot Mix; Summary of Mixable Types

micro_segments.title: Hakol yecholim lehitaarev chutz min hachatas veasham: all types can mix except Chatat and Asham (female vs. male distinction)

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Clarifying 'Afilu Echad Beribo' and Comparing Parallel Sugyot

micro_segments.title: Mai afilu: clarifying that one forbidden animal in ten thousand Korbanot requires all to die
Reference to Temurah: comparing parallel case where forbidden animals mixed with Chullin
Rav Ashi's question: why teach our case separately from the Temurah case?

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Why Both Cases Are Necessary; Distinguishing Issurei Hana'ah from Pasul Korban

micro_segments.title: If only Temurah case were taught, we might think restriction applies only to laypersons, not Kodshim use
If only our case were taught, we might think only Issurei Hana'ah (weighty) forbid, not C-section (lighter)
Gemara response: Takanata itztricha—our case is needed to show practical solutions (Mum, redemption)

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Establishing that Issurei Hana'ah Do Not Become Batul in Any Context

micro_segments.title: Referencing mishna on wine of idolatry and idols: Issurei Hana'ah are not batul even in smallest amounts
Requiring both cases to prevent conclusion that Bitul might apply to Kodshim or to Chullin contexts
Final principle: Issurei Hana'ah cannot become batul for Kodshim (respect for Kedusha) or for Chullin (layperson benefit)

topical_tags.term: Shor She'Ne'evda Bo Aveira (ox used in sin)
Nis'arvu Chullin Bi'Tmimim (Chullin mixed with Kodshim)
Kodshim bekodshim min bemino (Kodshim of same type mixed)
Kodshim bekodshim min beshe'eino mino (Kodshim of different types mixed)
Eating with Bechor restrictions (not in marketplace)
Afilu Echad Beribo (even one in ten thousand)
